stories.summary: In patients with HFrEF, AC01 over 28 days appeared safe and well tolerated, and no major harms were identified in this early-phase study. These findings support further investigations of AC01 in larger studies.

today.name: Today
today.detailedForecast: Patchy fog before 8am. Sunny, with a high near 93. Heat index values as high as 109. South wind 1 to 7 mph.

tonight.name: Tonight
tonight.detailedForecast: Mostly clear, with a low around 74. Heat index values as high as 106. South wind 2 to 6 mph.

weather.whatToWear: Lightweight linen or technical tee, breathable shorts, and a wide-brim hat — the heat index is brushing 109°F. Keep a water bottle within arm's reach and save the long outdoor work for early morning or after 7 PM.

life.oneThingToPlan: Block one 90-minute focus window on Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon this week — no calls, no email — and protect it. The week's loudest noise arrives Monday morning; a pre-committed quiet block is the cheapest gift you'll give yourself.

localPicksNextWeekend.title: Gateway Arch + Museum
localPicksNextWeekend.dateLabel: Daily; museum is the air-conditioned part of the day

localPicksNextWeekend.title: Missouri Botanical Garden (Shaw Nature Reserve)
localPicksNextWeekend.dateLabel: Daily, opens 7 AM — best before the heat
localPicksNextWeekend.venue: Gray Summit, MO (~30 min from Eureka)

rec.title: Howard Marks — The Most Important Thing (re-read, Ch. 18: 'The Most Important Thing')
rec.blurb: Pair with the week's market tape (Nasdaq -3.3%, BTC sub-$60K). Marks on second-level thinking is the right lens for a weekend where the obvious read was 'sell everything.' One chapter, re-read slowly, before Tuesday's open.

driveDistanceIdeas.name: Hermann / Augusta wine country
driveDistanceIdeas.blurb: River-town wineries with shaded patios. Augusta is America's first AVA — quiet July weekdays, livelier on weekends. An easy Saturday-morning drive from Eureka with cellar tours and lunch on the porch.

driveDistanceIdeas.name: Cahokia Mounds
driveDistanceIdeas.blurb: UNESCO site and an easy reflective stop — Monks Mound at sunset is bigger than you'd think. Walk the Woodhenge, read the small museum, drive home for dinner.

driveDistanceIdeas.name: Onondaga Cave State Park
driveDistanceIdeas.blurb: 57°F inside, year-round. If the July 4 weekend is hot, this is the antidote. One-hour guided tour through one of the most decorated caves in the US.

opportunities.title: BTC sub-$60K — first close below since Q3 2024
opportunities.blurb: Worth a quiet re-read of your BTC thesis before Tuesday. Not a signal to act, but the kind of price level where you want to know — clearly — whether your conviction changed or didn't.

opportunities.title: Q3 estimated-tax payment due September 15
opportunities.blurb: Block 30 min Mon AM to confirm YTD vs. safe harbor. Summer is when these deadlines quietly slip.

opportunities.title: Re-read Sonnet 4.5 release notes once more
opportunities.blurb: Frontier-model shifts move your retirement-business AI capability more than the week's news cycle. 30-min spike with a real delegate_task before Tuesday.

opportunities.title: Strength session, before the heat peaks
opportunities.blurb: Two 30-min compound-lift sessions this week move the 60+ longevity needle more than the cardio you've been sneaking in. Schedule them before Monday fills the week.

summaryBullets: What I learned this week: rate-path repricing drove the Nasdaq -3.3% and BTC sub-$60K in the same window — long-duration everything sold off together.
What I'm carrying into next week: a calmer read on the selloff (10Y only +10bps to 4.38%); the trade wasn't a thesis-breaker, it was a multiple compression.
What I'm setting down: altcoin noise and crypto-stock underperformance headlines. None of it changes the BTC exposure math.
What I'm picking back up: a Tuesday/Wednesday focus block, two compound-lift sessions, and a Monday beneficiary double-check on the inherited IRA.
What I'm watching Monday: the next FOMC minutes + a fresh CPI print — softer data unwinds the rate move.

actionItems.title: Calendar block: Mon 30 min — Q3 estimated-tax YTD review
actionItems.rationale: Sep 15 is closer than it feels.

actionItems.title: Calendar block: Tue or Wed 90 min — focus, no calls, no email
actionItems.rationale: Cheapest pre-Monday gift you can give yourself.

actionItems.title: Calendar block: 2x 30-min strength sessions this week
actionItems.rationale: Compound lifts, before the heat cancels plans.

actionItems.title: Calendar block: Mon 15 min — inherited IRA beneficiary check
actionItems.rationale: Sunday reflection lands a tiny Monday action.

funFact.fact: Bitcoin's first close below $60,000 since Q3 2024 came in the same week the Nasdaq dropped 3.3%. The correlation has been this tight since the spot-ETF era began — BTC trades with long-duration risk, not as a counterweight to it.

indicators.label: U.S. Dollar (DXY)
indicators.source: ICE US Dollar Index - Index - C

markets.whyParagraph: A stronger-than-expected June jobs report pushed the 10Y yield from 4.28% to 4.38% in five sessions, and long-duration everything repriced in sympathy: Nasdaq -3.3%, S&P -1.6%, BTC closed sub-$60K for the first time since Q3 2024. The dollar firmed slightly and the curve steepened (10Y -10bps to 4.38%, 30Y -10bps to 4.87% — relief at the long end is the quiet tell that the market read the jobs number as noise, not signal). Carrying into next week: the CPI print is the cleaner test. A softer read unwinds most of the rate move; a hot one keeps the multiple compression in place.

themes.displayName: Google is rationing Gemini capacity to Meta
themes.trendLine: The interesting story isn't the rationing — it's that AI compute is now a binding constraint at one of the largest buyers in the world. Reflective read: the AI-capex thesis just got another data point in its favor.

themes.displayName: NVDA — cooling momentum, narrative still looks undervalued
themes.trendLine: Two threads on the same name this week: 'near crucial support, buy signal?' from one source, 'cooling momentum' from another. Reflective read: the tape is split. Worth sitting with one more weekend before adding.

themes.displayName: Palantir — scaling this business takes more labor than the market realizes
themes.trendLine: Counterpoint to the AI-margin-expansion narrative. Worth reading alongside the Google/Meta capacity story — the next bottleneck after compute is implementation labor.

themes.displayName: MSFT — 35% drop framed as 'best gift in years'
themes.trendLine: Memeburn-style framing, but the underlying question is real: at what multiple does MSFT stop being a multiple-compression story and start being a value story? Reflective read: not yet, but the tape is moving in that direction.

themes.displayName: Meta — testing Arena, eyeing Polymarket / Kalshi partnerships
themes.trendLine: Prediction-market entry from a Mag7 name is the quiet story of the week. Worth thinking about alongside the Polymarket/CFTC headlines: the institutional rails are forming.

items.headline: BTC closed sub-$60K — first since Q3 2024
items.detail: Before Monday's open: re-read your BTC thesis in one paragraph. If the answer to 'why am I holding this' isn't immediately clear, that's the signal — not the price.

items.headline: 10Y at 4.38%, 30Y at 4.87% — curve steepened
items.detail: If you've been waiting on T-Bill ladder refills, this week's 10bps relief at the long end is your window. CPI print next week will set the next move.

items.headline: Q3 estimated-tax payment — Sep 15 deadline
items.detail: Calendar block Mon AM (30 min) — confirm YTD income vs. safe-harbor. Top up before Sep 15 to avoid Q4 penalties.

retirementWatch.weekEndReflection: Portfolio snapshot isn't loaded (TWS appears offline), so the week-end number isn't on the page — and that's fine. Sunday's reflection doesn't need a balance: confirm the beneficiaries on the inherited IRA are still right, scan the I-Bond rate for the next reset, and let the trade idea that's been sitting in your head sit one more weekend. The market will be there Monday.

entries.oneLiner: AI demand is outstripping supply — even Google can't keep up. Reflective read: the AI cap-ex thesis is now showing up in rationing, not just in earnings calls.
Stock opinions on Microsoft AI cap-ex worries — Quiver Quantitative roundup. The market is starting to discount the spend, but the spend is the moat.
AI writes a lot of software — now human code review is starting to disappear. Reflective read for the AI-business lane: reviewer role may be the next human-in-the-loop to compress.
Chinese AI closes in on U.S. cybersecurity models — the geopolitical layer is back in the AI trade. Worth one quiet read before next week.
Austria lobbies EU to host Anthropic AI after US curbs — sovereignty angle on the frontier-model stack. Quiet, but the kind of policy signal that compounds over years.
Oral GLP-1 (Orforglipron) beats SGLT2 inhibitor on HbA1c in head-to-head Phase 3. The oral GLP-1 era is real — ask your endocrinologist at the next visit.
Cliramitug (NI006-101 long-term follow-up) — monoclonal antibody that depletes cardiac amyloid transthyretin. Worth knowing about if cardiac amyloid is on your radar.
CagriSema (cagrilintide + semaglutide) on basal insulin — Phase 3 hit HbA1c + body weight endpoints. The combined GLP-1 / amylin class is the next weight + glucose story.
Oral ghrelin receptor agonist AC01 (GOAL-HF1) — Phase 1b/2a in HFrEF, safe + tolerated over 28 days. Early signal, but the oral HF med is the kind of long-tail read worth noting at this age.
Immersive VR + sensory neurostimulation outperformed conventional rehab for chronic-stroke upper-limb recovery. Quality-of-life read for the men-over-60 cohort.

articles.title: Howard Marks — 'The Most Important Thing' (Ch. 18)
articles.whyWorthIt: Re-read over the work week with this week's tape in mind. Second-level thinking on a Nasdaq -3.3% week is the right lens to bring into Tuesday's open.

articles.title: Anthropic — Claude Sonnet 4.5 release notes (re-read)
articles.whyWorthIt: Frontier-model shift that directly affects your retirement-business AI capability. 30-min spike before Wednesday — put one real delegate_task through it.

articles.title: JAMA / Nature Medicine — strength training for men over 60
articles.whyWorthIt: Synthesis of the recent cohort research on compound lifts. Decision-ready, not a fitness blog — schedule two 30-min sessions this week before the heat makes you skip.

videos.title: Lyn Alden — Bitcoin and the macro setup into Q3
videos.whyWorthIt: 45-min macro + on-chain read after the BTC sub-$60K close. Pairs well with the 'reflective' mood — let someone else's framework sit in your head for the week.

videos.title: The Daily podcast (Monday's episode, queued)
videos.whyWorthIt: Queue for the Tuesday morning commute — best 25 min summary of the week's setup.

podcasts.title: Acquired — latest episode (queue for next weekend)
podcasts.whyWorthIt: Long-form, decision-quality. Save for Saturday's drive or Sunday morning — the kind of read that informs the next month, not the next hour.
